message:
feat(conformance): time_ops executors time/clock/clock_gettime/localtime_r (bd-y1f9)

Adds four more arms to execute_fixture_case so the conformance
runner can execute fixtures for the POSIX time-of-day surface
without shelling out to hand-written unit tests.

crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:

- execute_time_case: calls frankenlibc_abi::time_abi::time(NULL)
  and records whether the returned time_t is positive ("POSITIVE_INT"
  as the canonical observation string) or the literal value when
  non-positive. Fixtures can match "POSITIVE_INT" to avoid wall-
  clock flakiness.
- execute_clock_case: calls clock() and records "NON_NEGATIVE"
  for the normal success range or the literal value otherwise,
  again so fixtures do not have to pin on an unpredictable CPU-
  time number.
- execute_clock_gettime_case: reads clk_id from the fixture
  inputs, zeroes a stack timespec, calls clock_gettime, and
  reports the numeric return so -1 / EINVAL / 0 can all be
  asserted exactly by the fixture.
- execute_localtime_r_case: reads time (i64) from inputs, zeroes
  a stack libc::tm, calls localtime_r, and records "NULL" /
  "TM_STRUCT" so fixtures can assert success vs failure without
  leaking the in-memory tm struct back into the fixture output.

All four helpers run through ensure_supported_mode(mode) + wrap
their observation in non_host_execution(...) so the existing
report pipeline treats them the same as every other entry. No
fixtures added here - this only registers the executors so
fixture authoring for bd-y1f9 can follow.
